• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Validation of {reappraised} for categorical (count) data

Ian Hussey

15 April, 2026

Dependencies

library(tibble)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(furrr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(reappraised)

Functions

generate_contingency_table <- function(p1, p2, n1, n2) {
  y1 <- rbinom(n1, 1, p1)
  y2 <- rbinom(n2, 1, p2)

  # 2x2 matrix: rows = outcome (0/1), cols = group (1/2)
  tab <- matrix(c(sum(y1 == 1), sum(y2 == 1),
                  sum(y1 == 0), sum(y2 == 0)),
                nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)

  return(tab)
}

compute_reference_pvalues <- function(tab) {
  tibble(
    p_chi_ref       = suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE)$p.value),
    p_chi_yates_ref = suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = TRUE)$p.value),
    p_fisher_ref    = fisher.test(tab)$p.value
  )
}

compute_reappraised_pvalues <- function(tab) {
  p_fisher <- reappraised:::fisher_fast(tab)

  chi_no_corr <- reappraised:::chi_fast(tab, correct = FALSE)
  chi_yates   <- reappraised:::chi_fast(tab, correct = TRUE)

  tibble(
    p_chi           = chi_no_corr$p,
    p_chi_yates     = chi_yates$p,
    p_fisher        = p_fisher,
    p_lr            = reappraised:::lr(tab),
    p_cmh           = reappraised:::cmh(tab),
    p_midp_exact    = reappraised:::midp_exact(tab),
    p_midp_classic  = reappraised:::midp_classic(tab, p_fisher),
    chi_warning     = chi_no_corr$warning
  )
}

run_single_sim <- function(p1, p2, n1, n2) {
  tab <- generate_contingency_table(p1, p2, n1, n2)

  # Flag degenerate tables (zero row/column sums)
  if (any(rowSums(tab) == 0) || any(colSums(tab) == 0)) {
    return(tibble(
      a = tab[1,1], b = tab[1,2], c = tab[2,1], d = tab[2,2],
      degenerate = TRUE
    ))
  }

  ref   <- compute_reference_pvalues(tab)
  reapp <- compute_reappraised_pvalues(tab)

  bind_cols(
    tibble(a = tab[1,1], b = tab[1,2], c = tab[2,1], d = tab[2,2],
           degenerate = FALSE),
    ref,
    reapp
  )
}

Run simulation

if (file.exists("simulation_count.rds")) {
  simulation <- read_rds("simulation_count.rds")
} else {

  # simulation parameters
  experiment_parameters <- expand_grid(
    pop_p1 = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5),
    pop_p2 = c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9),
    pop_n1 = c(10, 25, 50, 100),
    pop_n2 = c(10, 25, 50, 100),
    iteration = 1:500
  )

  # set up parallelization
  plan(multisession, workers = future::availableCores())

  # set seed
  set.seed(42)

  # run simulation
  simulation <- experiment_parameters |>
    mutate(results = future_pmap(
      .l = list(p1 = pop_p1, p2 = pop_p2, n1 = pop_n1, n2 = pop_n2),
      .f = run_single_sim,
      .progress = TRUE,
      .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
    )) |>
    unnest(results)

  write_rds(simulation, "simulation_count.rds", compress = "gz")

}

Degenerate tables

Tables with zero row or column margins (all successes or all failures in a group) cannot be tested. These are excluded from subsequent analyses.

simulation |>
  group_by(pop_p1, pop_p2, pop_n1, pop_n2) |>
  summarize(
    n_total = n(),
    n_degenerate = sum(degenerate),
    pct_degenerate = mean(degenerate) * 100
  ) |>
  filter(n_degenerate > 0) |>
  kable(digits = 1) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
pop_p1 pop_p2 pop_n1 pop_n2 n_total n_degenerate pct_degenerate
0.1 0.1 10 10 500 51 10.2
0.1 0.1 10 25 500 14 2.8
0.1 0.1 10 50 500 2 0.4
0.1 0.1 25 10 500 15 3.0
0.1 0.1 25 25 500 2 0.4
0.1 0.1 50 10 500 1 0.2
0.1 0.3 10 10 500 6 1.2
0.1 0.3 25 10 500 2 0.4
0.3 0.1 10 10 500 5 1.0
0.3 0.1 10 25 500 2 0.4
0.3 0.3 10 10 500 1 0.2 
# filter to non-degenerate for all subsequent analyses
simulation <- simulation |>
  filter(!degenerate) |>
  mutate(
    # absolute differences
    diff_chi       = abs(p_chi - p_chi_ref),
    diff_chi_yates = abs(p_chi_yates - p_chi_yates_ref),
    diff_fisher    = abs(p_fisher - p_fisher_ref),

    # exact matches (within floating point tolerance)
    match_chi       = near(p_chi, p_chi_ref, tol = 1e-10),
    match_chi_yates = near(p_chi_yates, p_chi_yates_ref, tol = 1e-10),
    match_fisher    = near(p_fisher, p_fisher_ref, tol = 1e-10),

    # expected cell warning flag
    has_chi_warning = (chi_warning != "")
  )

Overall agreement rates

Do reappraised’s internal functions produce identical p-values to R’s built-in chisq.test() and fisher.test()?

simulation |>
  summarize(
    n_sims               = n(),
    match_rate_chi        = mean(match_chi),
    match_rate_chi_yates  = mean(match_chi_yates),
    match_rate_fisher     = mean(match_fisher),
    max_diff_chi          = max(diff_chi),
    max_diff_chi_yates    = max(diff_chi_yates),
    max_diff_fisher       = max(diff_fisher)
  ) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
n_sims match_rate_chi match_rate_chi_yates match_rate_fisher max_diff_chi max_diff_chi_yates max_diff_fisher
119899 1 1 1 0 0 0

Agreement by conditions

By sample size

simulation |>
  group_by(pop_n1, pop_n2) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    match_rate_chi       = mean(match_chi),
    match_rate_chi_yates = mean(match_chi_yates),
    match_rate_fisher    = mean(match_fisher)
  ) |>
  kable(digits = 4) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
pop_n1 pop_n2 n match_rate_chi match_rate_chi_yates match_rate_fisher
10 10 7437 1 1 1
10 25 7484 1 1 1
10 50 7498 1 1 1
10 100 7500 1 1 1
25 10 7483 1 1 1
25 25 7498 1 1 1
25 50 7500 1 1 1
25 100 7500 1 1 1
50 10 7499 1 1 1
50 25 7500 1 1 1
50 50 7500 1 1 1
50 100 7500 1 1 1
100 10 7500 1 1 1
100 25 7500 1 1 1
100 50 7500 1 1 1
100 100 7500 1 1 1

By chi-squared expected cell warning

simulation |>
  group_by(has_chi_warning) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    match_rate_chi       = mean(match_chi),
    match_rate_chi_yates = mean(match_chi_yates),
    match_rate_fisher    = mean(match_fisher)
  ) |>
  kable(digits = 4) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
has_chi_warning n match_rate_chi match_rate_chi_yates match_rate_fisher
FALSE 61720 1 1 1
TRUE 58179 1 1 1

By population proportions

simulation |>
  group_by(pop_p1, pop_p2) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    match_rate_chi       = mean(match_chi),
    match_rate_chi_yates = mean(match_chi_yates),
    match_rate_fisher    = mean(match_fisher)
  ) |>
  kable(digits = 4) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
pop_p1 pop_p2 n match_rate_chi match_rate_chi_yates match_rate_fisher
0.1 0.1 7915 1 1 1
0.1 0.3 7992 1 1 1
0.1 0.5 8000 1 1 1
0.1 0.7 8000 1 1 1
0.1 0.9 8000 1 1 1
0.3 0.1 7993 1 1 1
0.3 0.3 7999 1 1 1
0.3 0.5 8000 1 1 1
0.3 0.7 8000 1 1 1
0.3 0.9 8000 1 1 1
0.5 0.1 8000 1 1 1
0.5 0.3 8000 1 1 1
0.5 0.5 8000 1 1 1
0.5 0.7 8000 1 1 1
0.5 0.9 8000 1 1 1

Scatter plots: reappraised vs. R built-in

simulation |>
  ggplot(aes(x = p_chi_ref, y = p_chi, color = match_chi)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.2, end = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "R chisq.test (reference)", y = "reappraised chi_fast",
       title = "Chi-squared test (no correction)") +
  theme_linedraw()

simulation |>
  ggplot(aes(x = p_chi_yates_ref, y = p_chi_yates, color = match_chi_yates)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.2, end = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "R chisq.test with Yates (reference)", y = "reappraised chi_fast with Yates",
       title = "Chi-squared test (Yates correction)") +
  theme_linedraw()

simulation |>
  ggplot(aes(x = p_fisher_ref, y = p_fisher, color = match_fisher)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.2, end = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "R fisher.test (reference)", y = "reappraised fisher_fast",
       title = "Fisher's exact test") +
  theme_linedraw()

Cross-test comparison

Distribution of p-values by test type across all simulations.

simulation |>
  slice_sample(n = min(5000, nrow(simulation))) |>
  select(p_chi, p_chi_yates, p_fisher, p_lr, p_cmh, p_midp_exact, p_midp_classic) |>
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "test", values_to = "p_value") |>
  mutate(test = factor(test, levels = c("p_chi", "p_chi_yates", "p_fisher",
                                         "p_lr", "p_cmh", "p_midp_exact", "p_midp_classic"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = p_value)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.02, boundary = 0) +
  facet_wrap(~ test, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of p-values by test type",
       x = "p-value", y = "Count")

Pairwise comparison: chi-squared vs Fisher’s exact.

simulation |>
  slice_sample(n = min(5000, nrow(simulation))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = p_chi, y = p_fisher)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Chi-squared (no correction)", y = "Fisher's exact",
       title = "Chi-squared vs Fisher's exact") +
  theme_linedraw()

simulation |>
  slice_sample(n = min(5000, nrow(simulation))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = p_chi, y = p_midp_exact)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "Chi-squared (no correction)", y = "Mid-p exact",
       title = "Chi-squared vs Mid-p exact") +
  theme_linedraw()

Test disagreement

Cases where different tests lead to different significance conclusions at alpha = .05.

test_disagreement <- simulation |>
  mutate(
    sig_chi       = p_chi < 0.05,
    sig_chi_yates = p_chi_yates < 0.05,
    sig_fisher    = p_fisher < 0.05,
    sig_midp      = p_midp_exact < 0.05,
    disagree_chi_fisher = sig_chi != sig_fisher,
    disagree_chi_yates  = sig_chi != sig_chi_yates,
    disagree_chi_midp   = sig_chi != sig_midp
  )

test_disagreement |>
  group_by(pop_p1, pop_p2) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    pct_disagree_chi_fisher = mean(disagree_chi_fisher) * 100,
    pct_disagree_chi_yates  = mean(disagree_chi_yates) * 100,
    pct_disagree_chi_midp   = mean(disagree_chi_midp) * 100
  ) |>
  filter(pct_disagree_chi_fisher > 0 | pct_disagree_chi_yates > 0 | pct_disagree_chi_midp > 0) |>
  kable(digits = 2) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
pop_p1 pop_p2 n pct_disagree_chi_fisher pct_disagree_chi_yates pct_disagree_chi_midp
0.1 0.1 7915 2.44 3.15 1.90
0.1 0.3 7992 7.32 12.66 4.07
0.1 0.5 8000 4.16 7.58 1.85
0.1 0.7 8000 1.68 2.08 0.38
0.1 0.9 8000 0.25 0.25 0.01
0.3 0.1 7993 7.54 12.27 4.04
0.3 0.3 7999 1.76 2.56 0.74
0.3 0.5 8000 6.48 9.11 3.04
0.3 0.7 8000 6.34 8.10 3.08
0.3 0.9 8000 1.86 2.33 0.49
0.5 0.1 8000 4.35 7.76 1.75
0.5 0.3 8000 6.44 9.10 2.62
0.5 0.5 8000 2.06 2.91 1.12
0.5 0.7 8000 6.25 9.07 2.61
0.5 0.9 8000 3.96 7.38 1.69

By sample size:

test_disagreement |>
  group_by(pop_n1, pop_n2) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    pct_disagree_chi_fisher = mean(disagree_chi_fisher) * 100,
    pct_disagree_chi_yates  = mean(disagree_chi_yates) * 100,
    pct_disagree_chi_midp   = mean(disagree_chi_midp) * 100
  ) |>
  kable(digits = 2) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)

Expected cell count warnings

Frequency of the chi-squared expected-cell-count < 5 warning, by sample size and population proportion.

simulation |>
  group_by(pop_p1, pop_p2, pop_n1) |>
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    pct_warning = mean(has_chi_warning) * 100
  ) |>
  filter(pct_warning > 0) |>
  kable(digits = 1) |>
  kable_classic(full_width = FALSE)
pop_p1 pop_p2 pop_n1 n pct_warning
